responsibilities: Program and configure industrial robots from manufacturers such as FANUC, ABB, and KUKA for welding, assembly, and material handling tasks
Design robotic work cells including fixturing, safety enclosures, and end-effector tooling to optimize cycle time and throughput
Develop and validate robot motion paths using teach pendant and offline programming software to meet production quality standards
Integrate vision systems and sensors with industrial robots to enable part inspection and pick-and-place accuracy
Collaborate with manufacturing engineers to design safety systems and interlocks that comply with industrial robot safety standards
Troubleshoot and optimize robot programs and PLC interfaces to resolve production line faults and minimize downtime
Conduct robot cell commissioning and run-off testing to validate cycle time, accuracy, and repeatability before production launch
Implement collaborative robot (cobot) solutions for tasks requiring close human-robot interaction on the production floor
Document robot programs, cell layouts, and maintenance procedures to support ongoing production operations and training
